excerpt: When Azrael arrived and began to gather the dust, the earth cried out, 'I adjure you by the glory of Allah who has sent you that you take nothing from my soil that will be destined for Hell.' Gabriel returned empty-handed and reported to Allah, 'The earth swore by Your honor, and I dared not disobey.' Allah then sent Michael, and the earth made the same plea; Michael too returned without taking anything. Finally, Allah sent Azrael, the Angel of Death.
source_location_hint: Narrative of creation of Adam from earth

excerpt: The Prophet replied: 'Ādam's forehead was fashioned from the clay of the Ka'bah, his ankles from Egyptian soil, his feet from the clay of the Hijāz, his right hand from the dust of the east, and his left hand from the dust of the west.'
source_location_hint: Hadith on Adam's creation from different soils

excerpt: This sacred earth was then kneaded with the water of Tasnīm, the spring of Paradise, until it became lustrous as pearl. The pearl was then immersed in all the springs of Paradise and bathed in their waters. When the gaze of Allah fell upon it, the pearl trembled with awe and shattered into one hundred twenty-four thousand drops, from each of which Allah created a Prophet.
source_location_hint: The creation of the prophets from Muhammad's light

excerpt: Imam Jaʿfar al-Ṣādiq transmits from his father concerning what the angels revealed versus what they concealed
The narrative of Iblīs examining Ādam's form and his secret oath to disobey
The angels' repentance and the establishment of the Kaʿba on earth, raised to the fourth heaven before Noah's flood
Iblīs's claim of superiority: 'I am better than him. You created me from fire and him from clay'
The description of Paradise as the station of prophethood connected to revelation, pure and subtle in nature
Iblīs's vow: 'I shall destroy the descendants of Ādam born until the reappearance of the Qaʾim, all but a few'
God's acceptance of Iblīs's challenge and the establishment of the two callings: merciful and satanic
Satan's false oath and the deception of Ādam and Ḥawwāʾ; the doctrine that falsehood had no place in their consciousness
If the tree whose fruit was forbidden to Adam was good and lawful, why was Adam forbidden to eat its fruit? And if that tree was forbidden, then how could it exist in Paradise?
source_location_hint: Chapter on Hazrat Adam (peace be upon him)

excerpt: The tree represents the knowledge of realities (ʿilm al-ḥaqāʾiq) which leads to eternity and liberates the soul, yet cannot be acquired without obedience to the Imāms and allegiance to them.
source_location_hint: Esoteric exegesis of the forbidden tree

excerpt: In an exalted sense, the tree signifies knowledge concerning the hierarchy of the Qāʾim and the Amīr al-Muʾminīn ʿAlī, mysteries hidden from countless multitudes and forbidden to Ādam because he stood at the threshold of the first cycle.

In another sense, the tree itself was Iblīs, who was a mighty teacher of all the angels. The prohibition upon eating the fruit means Ādam was forbidden to disclose to Iblīs any secrets of the daʿwa, for Iblīs had become accursed through disobedience.
source_location_hint: Iblīs as symbolic representation of the tree

excerpt: A prophet cannot be an oppressor. Ādam possessed a group of sinners upon whom this punishment was to be enacted. His lower veil descended to the islands; his higher veil remained among the nobles. In every cycle there appears an Ādam, and he too errs.
source_location_hint: Resolution of prophetic infallibility and Ādam's error

excerpt: Those Words were the blessed names of the Panjatan Pāk: Muḥammad, ʿAlī, Fāṭima, al-Ḥasan, and al-Ḥusayn—peace be upon them all. Through these sacred names, Ādam repented and sought forgiveness.
source_location_hint: Spiritual meaning of Ādam's repentance and the five holy names

excerpt: My servants—over them you shall have no power except those who follow you from among the wayward (Quran 15:42). Satan was vanquished, and Allāh's promise was fulfilled.
source_location_hint: Affirmation of Satan's limited authority over the believers
